seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts/achieved.
mutation: composite: recast frequency assignment as weighted minimum-conflict C-labeling on a themed tide-pool overflow graph (min monochromatic edge weight = Max-C-Cut complement), and generalize the input from a geometric interference graph to an arbitrary weighted multigraph with heavy-tailed mixing strengths and shelf community structure, so the optimum is NP-hard and greedy/local-search/multi-restart heuristics diverge.
title: Tide Pool Niche Partition: Minimizing Competitive Overlap
strategies: do-nothing baseline: seed every pool with niche 1 (all channels monochromatic -> equals baseline B, ratio 0.1)
sequential greedy labeling: scan pools 1..n and assign each the niche minimizing added conflict weight to already-labeled neighbors
local-move descent: from a greedy start, repeatedly relabel any pool to the niche minimizing conflict over all its neighbors until no single move improves
seeded multi-restart randomized descent: run local-move descent from several randomized initial labelings and keep the lowest-conflict assignment found
